title: Pythagorean theorem — geometric proof
hint: drag the slider · or press play

steps.title: The claim
steps.body: For a right triangle with legs $a$, $b$ and hypotenuse $c$: $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$.
steps.svgInner: <polygon points='40,200 200,200 200,40' fill='var(--panel2)' stroke='var(--ink)' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='115' y='220' text-anchor='middle' font-size='14' fill='var(--mute)'>a</text><text x='215' y='125' text-anchor='middle' font-size='14' fill='var(--mute)'>b</text><text x='100' y='110' text-anchor='middle' font-size='14' fill='var(--yellow)'>c</text>

steps.title: Tile a square of side $a+b$ two ways
steps.body: Take a big square of side length $a+b$ and place four copies of the right triangle in two arrangements. The leftover area must agree across both arrangements.
steps.svgInner: <rect x='40' y='40' width='160' height='160' fill='none' stroke='var(--ink)' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='40' y1='80' x2='200' y2='80' stroke='var(--mute)' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><line x1='160' y1='40' x2='160' y2='200' stroke='var(--mute)' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/>

steps.title: Arrangement A: two squares $a^2 + b^2$
steps.body: In the first arrangement the leftover region is a square of side $a$ plus a square of side $b$ — total area $a^2 + b^2$.
steps.svgInner: <rect x='40' y='40' width='40' height='40' fill='color-mix(in srgb, var(--blue) 25%, var(--panel2))' stroke='var(--blue)'/><rect x='80' y='80' width='120' height='120' fill='color-mix(in srgb, var(--green) 25%, var(--panel2))' stroke='var(--green)'/><text x='60' y='65' text-anchor='middle' font-size='14' fill='var(--ink)'>a²</text><text x='140' y='145' text-anchor='middle' font-size='14' fill='var(--ink)'>b²</text>

steps.title: Arrangement B: one square $c^2$
steps.body: In the second arrangement the leftover region is a single tilted square whose side is the hypotenuse $c$ — total area $c^2$.
steps.svgInner: <polygon points='80,40 200,80 160,200 40,160' fill='color-mix(in srgb, var(--yellow) 25%, var(--panel2))' stroke='var(--yellow)' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='120' y='128' text-anchor='middle' font-size='16' fill='var(--ink)'>c²</text>

steps.title: Equate
steps.body: Both arrangements have the same total area $(a+b)^2$ and the same four triangles removed, so the leftover regions are equal: $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$.
steps.svgInner: <text x='120' y='130' text-anchor='middle' font-size='22' fill='var(--yellow)' font-weight='600'>a² + b² = c²</text>
